creator: george@m5p.com
text:
Created attachment 202660
Allow kicad-devel configure to find python 2.7 instead of 2.6.

I recently changed the default Python version to 3.6 after having stuck with 2.7 for long enough (probably too long) -- but 2.7 is still installed.  However, kicad-devel fruitlessly searches for version 2.6, finds 3.6, and gives up in the configuration phase.  The attached patch solves the problem.  (I wouldn't be surprised if this also applies to kicad as well as kicad-devel.)
